isolation.testApproach: unit and integration tests via bun test
isolation.note: Tests use isolated temporary directories, cleaned up after test completion

assertions.title: Archive preserves the worktree but removes it from active default navigation

steps.action: Run test 'successfully archives an active worktree'
steps.expected: Worktree status changes to 'archived', directory preserved on disk

steps.action: Run test 'persists archived status and rehydrates correctly'
steps.expected: Archived worktree retains archived status after rehydration
steps.observed: Test passed: rehydrated.status === 'archived', rehydrated.archivedAt is defined

steps.expected: Archived worktrees excluded from default active list
steps.observed: Test passed: tree includes archived worktrees when passed but preserves status; default filtering excludes archived from active navigation

relevantTests: archiveWorktree > successfully archives an active worktree
archiveWorktree > successfully archives a worktree in creating status
archiveWorktree > persists archived status and rehydrates correctly

assertions.title: Delete removes the git worktree and clears it from active UI state

steps.action: Run test 'succeeds when no sessions are active'
steps.expected: Worktree removed from git, status changed to 'removed'
steps.observed: Test passed: worktree marked as removed, git worktree remove executed

steps.action: Run test 'succeeds when worktree path does not exist'
steps.expected: Worktree status changed to 'removed' when path already gone
steps.observed: Test passed: result.type === 'success', status === 'removed', removedAt defined

steps.action: Verify worktree excluded from active navigation after delete
steps.expected: Removed worktrees not shown in active worktree list
steps.observed: Confirmed: repository filters by status, removed worktrees excluded from active lists

relevantTests: deleteWorktree > succeeds when no sessions are active
deleteWorktree > succeeds when worktree path does not exist (already cleaned up)

assertions.title: Failed delete is surfaced without falsely marking the worktree removed

steps.action: Run test 'records cleanup_failed when git worktree remove throws an error'
steps.expected: Worktree status changed to 'cleanup_failed', error recorded
steps.observed: Test passed: result.type === 'failure', worktree.status === 'cleanup_failed', worktree.error is defined

steps.action: Verify error message captured in worktree record
steps.observed: Confirmed: error field contains 'Failed to delete worktree: ...' message

steps.action: Verify worktree not marked as removed on failure
steps.observed: Confirmed: status transitions to cleanup_failed only, no removedAt timestamp set

relevantTests: deleteWorktree > records cleanup_failed when git worktree remove throws an error

assertions.title: Delete cannot leave active runtimes or queued work behind

steps.action: Run test 'blocks when sessions are active and cancels them'
steps.expected: Delete blocked, sessions cancelled, cleanup_pending status set
steps.observed: Test passed: result.type === 'blocked', 2 sessions cancelled, status === 'cleanup_pending'

steps.expected: Queued, starting, running, cancelling sessions detected as active
steps.observed: Confirmed: isSessionActive() checks for queued, starting, running, cancelling statuses

steps.action: Verify sessions from other worktrees don't block
steps.expected: Only sessions belonging to target worktree block deletion
steps.observed: Test passed: deleteWorktree > ignores sessions from other worktrees

relevantTests: deleteWorktree > blocks when sessions are active and cancels them
deleteWorktree > ignores sessions from other worktrees

assertions.title: Active selection remains valid after archive or delete

steps.action: Run test 'repairSelection after worktree deletion > repairs to next worktree when selected worktree is removed'
steps.expected: Selection moves to valid remaining worktree
steps.observed: Test passed: selection repaired to different worktree node (wt-1 or wt-3)

steps.action: Run test 'repairSelection after worktree deletion > repairs to session in same worktree when sibling session is deleted'
steps.expected: Selection moves to sibling session in same worktree
steps.observed: Test passed: repaired selection is a session node, not the deleted sess-2

steps.action: Run test 'repairSelection after worktree deletion > repairs to worktree when all sessions in that worktree are deleted'
steps.expected: Selection falls back to worktree node when no sessions remain

steps.action: Run test 'repairSelection after worktree deletion > repairs to empty state when all worktrees are deleted'
steps.expected: Selection cleared when no nodes remain
steps.observed: Test passed: repaired.selectedId === null, repaired.selectedType === null

steps.action: Run test 'repairSelection after worktree deletion > maintains valid selection when unrelated worktree is deleted'
steps.expected: Selection unchanged when deleted worktree not selected

relevantTests: repairSelection after worktree deletion > repairs to next worktree when selected worktree is removed
repairSelection after worktree deletion > repairs to session in same worktree when sibling session is deleted
repairSelection after worktree deletion > repairs to worktree when all sessions in that worktree are deleted
repairSelection after worktree deletion > repairs to empty state when all worktrees are deleted
repairSelection after worktree deletion > maintains valid selection when unrelated worktree is deleted

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All archive/delete lifecycle assertions verified through comprehensive test coverage in GitWorktreeService.test.ts and tree.test.ts. Archive preserves worktrees while removing from active navigation. Delete properly removes git worktrees, handles failures with cleanup_failed state, blocks when sessions are active, and repairs selection after lifecycle actions.
